pi_items.metadata_value: Creates a 3D relief effect by emphasizing edges and converting image to grayscale
pi_items.verified_value: GIMP emboss: 'Bright areas are raised and dark ones are carved. It stamps and carves the active layer or selection, giving it relief with bumps and hollows. This changes the colors in your image to grayscale'

pi_items.metadata_value: Azimuth, Elevation, and Depth parameters (mentioned in README section 4)
pi_items.verified_value: Azimuth (0-360°), Elevation (0-180°), Depth - all confirmed in official GIMP 2.10 documentation

pi_items.metadata_value: Uses statistical image analysis: edge enhancement, texture increase, saturation reduction, overall pixel change
pi_items.verified_value: Verifier.py implements these exact metrics in functions: calculate_edge_enhancement(), calculate_texture_enhancement(), calculate_saturation_reduction(), calculate_overall_change()

pi_items.metadata_value: 75% (requires clear emboss effect with good quality)
GIMP 2.10 (based on config directory creation in setup scripts)
pi_items.verified_value: Setup script creates .config/GIMP/2.10 directories, libgimp2.0-dev installed
export_embosh.sh line 27 types 'embossed_image', verifier.py looks for /home/ga/Desktop/embossed_image.jpg
pi_items.source: Code analysis of export_emboss.sh and verifier.py

privileged_info_summary: The emboss_filter@1 task applies GIMP's Emboss filter (found in Filters → Distorts menu, not Artistic) to real portrait images from Unsplash (photo IDs 1507003211169-0a1dd7228f2d and 1500648767791-00dcc994a43e, each ~687x1030 pixels). The emboss effect converts images to grayscale with 3D relief appearance using azimuth/elevation/depth parameters. Verification uses statistical metrics (edge enhancement, texture increase, saturation reduction) with a 75% pass threshold. Expected output: embossed_image.jpg on Desktop.
